workflow.critical: The workflow execution engine is governed by: {project-root}/~/.nakiros/core/tasks/workflow.xml
You MUST have already loaded and processed: {installed_path}/workflow.yaml
YOU MUST ALWAYS SPEAK OUTPUT in the configured communication_language.
Write confidence artifacts in document_language. Keep internal keys and machine fields in English.
Every confidence claim must be backed by concrete evidence (file path, command output, or observable repository signal).
This workflow is strict: confidence is meaningful only when ALL repos are covered.
Execute ALL steps in exact order; do NOT skip steps.

step.action: Load project config from {project_config} when present (optional, warn if missing and continue).
Load profile config from {profile_config} when available (optional).
Build effective config as: profile config (base) + project config (override).
Apply defaults when missing: communication_language={default_communication_language}, document_language={default_document_language}.

step.critical: Confidence reports must be written in the workspace control root (.nakiros global), not in an app repo.
step.action: Set {{execution_repository_mode}} = "workspace-control".
Set {{confidence_artifact_repo_path}} = {project-root}.
Set {{confidence_steps_dir}} = {{confidence_artifact_repo_path}}/.nakiros/workflows/steps.
Set {{confidence_workspace_dir}} = {{confidence_artifact_repo_path}}/.nakiros/workspace/confidence.
step.output: [CONFIDENCE] ✓ Execution repository mode: workspace-control (enforced) | Artifacts repo: {{confidence_artifact_repo_path}}

step.action: Check for {workspace_config}. If found, load all repos from workspace.repos (name, role, localPath).

check.ask: No .nakiros.workspace.yaml found at {workspace_config}. Provide a workspace yaml path, or continue in single-repo mode. (path/single)

check.action: Load workspace config from provided path and extract workspace.repos.

step.action: Set {{is_multi_repo}} = true and {{repos_to_analyze}} = all workspace repos.
Set {{is_multi_repo}} = false and {{repos_to_analyze}} = [{project-root}] as single repo scope.
Set {{primary_repo}} as first repo in {{repos_to_analyze}} (or project-root for single repo mode).
step.critical: You MUST analyze every repo in {{repos_to_analyze}}. No partial scan.
Do not mark a repo as covered unless ALL mandatory categories have evidence with concrete file references.

action.action: Category A - Required tech and skills: - Detect stack from manifests (package.json, requirements.txt, Cargo.toml, go.mod, pom.xml, etc.). - Build required skills list (frameworks, language runtime, DB, infra, testing framework). - Collect available skill/capability signals (local skills, _nakiros agents/workflows, PM integration, MCP/tooling setup).
Category B - Functional understanding: - Summarize what this repo does in the project (2-5 concrete bullets). - Back each statement with file references (README/docs + key implementation files).

Category C - Documentation and ticketing readiness: - Verify existence/quality of docs needed to create good tickets (domain flows, architecture, AC references, constraints, glossary). - Mark missing docs that prevent reliable story/ticket elaboration.

Category D - Testability readiness: - Identify how to run tests (unit/integration/e2e) and prerequisites. - Detect test frameworks, scripts, fixtures, env files, and CI test jobs. - Capture exact runnable commands when available.
Category E - Operational readiness: - Verify CI/CD and deployment/rollback clues (pipelines, Dockerfile, infra manifests, runbooks). - Note what is missing to deploy safely.

Store per-repo evidence record {{repo_evidence_N}} with: - repo_name, role, localPath - required_skills - available_capabilities - functional_understanding_summary - docs_ticketing_findings - testability_findings - operational_findings - missing_categories (if any)

step.critical: Coverage gate must pass before allowing a high-confidence verdict.
step.action: Build {{repo_coverage_matrix}} with one row per repo and these columns: - repo - category_A_skills (OK/MISSING) - category_B_understanding (OK/MISSING) - category_C_docs_ticketing (OK/MISSING) - category_D_testability (OK/MISSING) - category_E_operability (OK/MISSING) - key_missing_items
Compute {{repos_fully_covered}} = count of repos where all five categories are OK.
Compute {{repo_coverage_percent}} = (repos_fully_covered / repos_to_analyze.length) * 100.

check.action: Add a critical blocker: "Not all repos are fully covered. Confidence cannot be considered meaningful yet."

step.critical: All scores are 0-100 and must be justified with evidence and repository coverage status.
step.action: Score `skill_coverage` (0-100): required tech skills vs available agent/automation capabilities.
Score `project_understanding` (0-100): how clearly AI can explain what each repo does and how they connect.
Score `documentation_ticket_readiness` (0-100): whether docs are sufficient to write high-quality stories/tickets.
Score `testability_readiness` (0-100): whether AI can run tests reliably with known commands/prerequisites.
Score `operational_readiness` (0-100): whether delivery/deploy/release signals are sufficient for safe assistance.

Compute weighted global score as: ((skill_coverage * {weight_skill_coverage}) + (project_understanding * {weight_project_understanding}) + (documentation_ticket_readiness * {weight_documentation_ticket_readiness}) + (testability_readiness * {weight_testability_readiness}) + (operational_readiness * {weight_operational_readiness})) / 100 Save as {{raw_global_confidence_score}}.
Apply coverage cap: {{global_confidence_score}} = min({{raw_global_confidence_score}}, {{global_score_cap}}).

step.goal: Generate prioritized user actions to make AI highly effective
step.action: Build {{critical_gaps}} list (max 10) for blockers that prevent trustworthy AI assistance.
Build {{recommended_gaps}} list (max 10) for high-value improvements.

Generate {{action_backlog}} with actionable items sorted by impact: - priority: P0 / P1 / P2 - action_title - why_it_matters - affected_repos - concrete_user_action - verification (command or file to check) - estimated_effort (S/M/L) - expected_score_gain
Split backlog into: - {{quick_wins}}: actions expected to be done in less than one day - {{structural_actions}}: deeper changes
Generate {{confidence_boost_plan}}: smallest set of actions likely to increase confidence by +20 points.

check.action: Set {{assistant_mode}} = "Do not rely on AI for full-project decisions yet; complete missing repo coverage first.".
Set {{assistant_mode}} = "AI can assist across the project (ticket elaboration + implementation guidance + test guidance) with normal validation checks.".

check.if: not coverage_gate_failed and global_confidence_score < score_threshold_confident and global_confidence_score >= score_threshold_partial
check.action: Set {{assistant_mode}} = "AI assistance is useful but must flag uncertainty and request validation on weaker dimensions.".
Set {{assistant_mode}} = "Focus on documentation/skills/testability gaps before relying on AI for delivery decisions.".

step.action: Ensure directories exist: {{confidence_steps_dir}} and {{confidence_workspace_dir}}.

Set report paths: - {{confidence_report_md}} = {{confidence_workspace_dir}}/project-understanding-confidence-{{date}}.md - {{confidence_report_json}} = {{confidence_workspace_dir}}/project-understanding-confidence-{{date}}.json - {{workflow_snapshot_md}} = {{confidence_steps_dir}}/project-understanding-confidence-{{date}}.md

Write JSON report to {{confidence_report_json}} with machine-readable fields: { "date": "{{date}}", "coverage_gate_failed": {{coverage_gate_failed}}, "repo_coverage_percent": {{repo_coverage_percent}}, "raw_score": {{raw_global_confidence_score}}, "score": {{global_confidence_score}}, "verdict": "{{confidence_verdict}}", "assistant_mode": "{{assistant_mode}}", "execution_repository_mode": "{{execution_repository_mode}}", "confidence_artifact_repo_path": "{{confidence_artifact_repo_path}}", "dimensions": { "skill_coverage": {{skill_coverage}}, "project_understanding": {{project_understanding}}, "documentation_ticket_readiness": {{documentation_ticket_readiness}}, "testability_readiness": {{testability_readiness}}, "operational_readiness": {{operational_readiness}} }, "repo_coverage_matrix": {{repo_coverage_matrix}}, "critical_gaps": {{critical_gaps}}, "recommended_gaps": {{recommended_gaps}}, "action_backlog": {{action_backlog}} }
Write a concise workflow snapshot to {{workflow_snapshot_md}} (same key results, short format).
Load and execute validation checklist from {validation}.
If checklist fails, go back to steps 4-9 to fix missing evidence/scoring/reporting details.
